Details:
A study evaluating the adsorption capacity of GAC in distilled water and river water found that the Freundlich K values varied significantly depending on the source water and pH value.  GAC is the Best Available Technology for glyphosate control. 
The greatest adsorption capacity was achieved with spiked lab water in slightly acidic conditions. The lowest adsorption capacity, on the other hand, was found with surface water in slightly acidic conditions. Interestingly, spiked lab water investigated at a basic pH of 10 achieved better adsorption than the surface water, but much less adsorption than the slightly acidic lab water solution. This suggests adsorption capacity is a function of pH, but is also dependent on source water characteristics.
